basic_details.job_title: Delhi Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline for 62 Assistant cum Data Entry Operator, Accountant and More Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Department of Women and Child Development (State Child Protection Society – SCPS), Government of NCT of Delhi, ISBT Building, Kashmiri Gate, Delhi – 110006
basic_details.post_name: Programme Officer (SCPS), District Child Protection Officer, Protection Officer (Institutional Care), Protection Officer (Non-Institutional Care), Legal cum Probation Officer, Counselor, Social Worker, Accountant, Data Analyst, Assistant cum Data Entry Operator, Out Reach Worker
basic_details.job_type: Purely contractual basis - initially for a period of one year; NOT a permanent job with Government of NCT of Delhi or Government of India
basic_details.short_information: Department of WCD, GNCTD is hiring contractual staff under Mission Vatsalya Scheme across 10 post categories in SCPS and DCPUs, last date 26 June 2026.

important_dates.shortlisted candidates list: To be displayed on the Department website (https://www.wcd.delhi.gov.in)
important_dates.interview_date: To be notified on Department website; shortlisted candidates also informed via E-mail

application_fee.details: No application fee details are specified in the notification. The application process is conducted through the online portal http://cams.wcddel.in. Only online applications shall be entertained by the Department of WCD.

age_limit.details: The applicant must meet the age requirement specified in the Terms of Reference for each post and should not exceed this age as of the application closing date (26-06-2026). Further age relaxation will be provided to applicants already working in the Department of WCD, GNCTD on the basis of their departmental experience. Reservation as per Delhi State Government rules is applicable.

post_wise_qualification.essential_qualification: Post Graduate degree in Social Work / Sociology / Child Development / Human Rights / Public Administration / Psychology / Psychiatry / Law / Public Health / Community Resource Management (min. 50%) from recognized University; 12th pass with min. 60% marks; minimum 2 years of post-qualification R&D/NGO/Government experience in Documentation, Training & Capacity Building, Project Formulation/Implementation, Monitoring & Supervision preferably in Women & Child Development/Social Welfare. Computer skills; fluency in English & Hindi.

Post Graduate degree in Social Work / Sociology / Child Development / Human Rights / Public Administration / Psychology / Psychiatry / Law / Public Health / Community Resource Management (min. 50%) from recognized University; 12th pass with min. 60%; minimum 3 years of post-qualification experience in Government/NGO in Documentation, Training & Capacity Building, Project Formulation/Implementation, Monitoring & Supervision preferably in Women & Child Development/Social Welfare. Computer skills; fluency in English & Hindi.

Post Graduate degree in Social Work / Sociology / Child Development / Human Rights / Public Administration / Psychology / Psychiatry / Law / Public Health / Community Resource Management (min. 50%) from recognized University; 12th pass with min. 60%; Computer skills; fluency in English & Hindi. OR Graduate in relevant disciplines (min. 50%) + 2 years' post-graduation experience in Women & Child Development/Social Welfare; 12th with min. 60%.

Post Graduate degree in Social Work / Sociology / Child Development / Human Rights / Public Administration / Psychology / Psychiatry / Law / Public Health / Community Resource Management (min. 50%); 12th pass with min. 60%; Computer skills; fluency in English & Hindi. OR Graduate in relevant disciplines (min. 50%) + 2 years' post-graduation experience in Women & Child Development/Social Welfare; 12th with min. 60%.

LLB from recognized University; 12th pass with min. 60%; minimum 2 years post-qualification experience in Government/NGO preferably in Women & Child Rights; good understanding of child rights & protection issues; proficiency in computer skills & writing; fluency in English & Hindi.

Graduate (B.A.) in Social Work / Sociology / Psychology / Public Health / Counseling (min. 50%) from recognized University; 12th with min. 60%; minimum 1 year post-qualification experience in Government/NGO in Women & Child Development. OR PG Diploma in Counseling and Communication; 12th with min. 60%; minimum 1 year experience. Computer skills; fluency in English & Hindi.

Graduate (B.A.) in Social Work / Sociology / Social Sciences (min. 50%) from recognized University; 12th with min. 60%; minimum 1 year post-qualification experience in Government/NGO in Women & Child Development; computer skills; fluency in English & Hindi.

Graduate in Commerce / Mathematics (min. 50%) from recognized University; 12th with min. 60%; minimum 1 year post-qualification experience in desired field; computer skills including command on Tally; fluency in English & Hindi.

Graduation in Statistics / Mathematics / Economics / Computer (BCA) (min. 50%) from recognized University; 12th with min. 60%; minimum 1 year post-qualification experience in Women & Child Development; knowledge of Hindi & English.

12th pass from recognized Board (min. 60%); 1-Year Diploma/Certificate in Computer Applications from recognized institute/university; minimum typing speed of 35 Words Per Minute (WPM) in English on computer; minimum 1 year experience in Women & Child Development.
12th pass from recognized school (min. 60%); good communication skills; knowledge of Hindi & English; minimum 1 year experience in Women & Child Development; basic knowledge of computers.

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions including age, educational qualification, and experience as specified in the Terms of References (TORs) for each post. The age must not exceed the limit specified in the TOR for each post as of the application closing date (26-06-2026). Relevant experience will be counted after attainment of the essential qualification (i.e., experience gained during graduation/post-graduation will not be counted as post-qualification experience). Candidates currently working in WCD, GNCTD will get additional age relaxation based on departmental experience.

DISTRICT CHILD PROTECTION UNITS (DCPUs) / JUVENILE JUSTICE BOARDS / CHILD WELFARE COMMITTEES / SPECIAL JUVENILE POLICE UNITS

selection_process: the list of eligible candidates will be displayed on the Department website (https://www.wcd.delhi.gov.in). Only shortlisted candidates will be called for interview. The decision of the Selection Committee shall be final and binding. Shortlisted candidates will also be informed through E-mail. The department's notice board and official website shall be referred for all updates. No correspondence will be entertained after the interview decision.

Applications will be scrutinized for eligibility. The list of eligible shortlisted candidates will be displayed on the Department website. Candidates are advised to regularly check https://www.wcd.delhi.gov.in for the latest updates.

Only shortlisted candidates will be called for interview. The decision of the Selection Committee is final and binding. Shortlisted candidates will be informed through E-mail as well as through the Department's notice board and official website.
how_to_apply: Only online applications shall be entertained by the Department of WCD. Candidates may apply online through http://cams.wcddel.in from 05-06-2026 (12:01 AM) to 26-06-2026 (11:59 PM). For detailed Terms of References (TORs), essential qualifications, eligibility criteria, and experience requirements for each post, visit the Department website: https://www.wcd.delhi.gov.in. Candidates are advised to apply well before the last date.
Documents / Information Required at the time of Online Application / Interview:
Educational qualification certificates and mark sheets (from 12th onwards, including graduation/post-graduation degree, as applicable per post)
LLB degree certificate (for Legal cum Probation Officer post)
PG Diploma in Counseling and Communication certificate (if applicable for Counselor post)
1-Year Diploma/Certificate in Computer Applications (for ADEO post)
Typing speed certificate (minimum 35 WPM in English on computer - for ADEO post)
Experience certificates from previous employers certifying post-qualification experience (specifying period, nature of work, and whether regular paid capacity)
Age proof (10th certificate or birth certificate)
Category certificate - SC/ST/OBC/EWS as applicable (as per Delhi State Government format)
Identity proof (Aadhaar Card, Voter ID, etc.)
NOC from present employer (for candidates currently employed in Government/WCD, GNCTD)
For TORs and further details: https://www.wcd.delhi.gov.in
important_instructions: This recruitment is purely on a contractual basis, initially for a period of one year. It is NOT a permanent job with the Government of NCT of Delhi or the Government of India. A candidate shall be eligible to apply for only one post. If a candidate applies for more than one post, ALL applications submitted by the candidate shall be summarily rejected. The Department reserves the right to decrease or increase the number of posts in each category mentioned in the table at any stage/point of time. The Department further reserves the right to terminate this contract work at any stage/point of time. Reservation is applicable as per Delhi State Government rules. The applicant must meet the age requirement specified in the TOR for each post and should not exceed this age as of the application closing date (26-06-2026). Further age relaxation will be provided to applicants already working in the Department of WCD, GNCTD on the basis of their departmental experience. Only shortlisted candidates will be called for interview. The decision of the Selection Committee is final and binding. After scrutiny, the list of eligible candidates will be displayed on the Department website (https://www.wcd.delhi.gov.in). Candidates are advised to check the website regularly. Shortlisted candidates will be informed through E-mail as well as through the Department's notice board and official website. Only online applications shall be entertained by the Department of WCD. No offline/physical applications will be accepted. TORs, essential qualifications, eligibility criteria, experience required, and job details are available on the Department website (https://www.wcd.delhi.gov.in).

faq.question: Q1. What is Mission Vatsalya Scheme and what posts are available under this Delhi WCD Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Mission Vatsalya is a flagship scheme of the Ministry of Women and Child Development, Government of India, aimed at child protection and welfare. Under this scheme, the Department of Women and Child Development, Government of NCT of Delhi has announced contractual vacancies for 10 post categories: Programme Officer (SCPS), District Child Protection Officer (DCPO), Protection Officer (Institutional Care), Protection Officer (Non-Institutional Care), Legal cum Probation Officer, Counselor, Social Worker, Accountant, Data Analyst, Assistant cum Data Entry Operator (ADEO), and Out Reach Worker — for placement in SCPS, DCPUs, JJBs, CWCs, and SJPUs across Delhi.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for Mission Vatsalya Delhi WCD Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for submission of online applications is 26-06-2026 (by 11:59 PM). The application process commenced on 05-06-2026 (12:01 AM). Applications must be submitted online only through http://cams.wcddel.in. No offline or physical applications will be accepted. Candidates are advised to apply well before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical issues.

faq.question: Q3. What is the salary for various posts under Mission Vatsalya Delhi WCD Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The consolidated monthly remuneration varies by post: DCPO — Rs. 44,023/-; Programme Officer — Rs. 34,755/-; Protection Officers (IC & NIC) and Legal cum Probation Officer — Rs. 27,804/-; Counselor, Social Worker, Accountant, and Data Analyst — Rs. 24,356/-; ADEO and Out Reach Worker — Rs. 22,411/-. Posts marked * are as per Mission Vatsalya Scheme; posts marked ** are as per Minimum Wages applicable in Delhi.

faq.question: Q4. Is Mission Vatsalya Delhi WCD 2026 a permanent government job?
faq.answer: No. This recruitment is purely on a contractual basis, initially for a period of one year. It is explicitly stated in the notification that this is NOT a permanent job with the Government of NCT of Delhi or the Government of India. The Department also reserves the right to terminate the contract at any stage/point of time. There is no assurance of regularization or extension after the initial one-year period.

faq.question: Q5. Can I apply for more than one post in Mission Vatsalya Delhi WCD Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: No. A candidate is eligible to apply for only one post. This is explicitly stated in the notification: "In case a candidate applies for more than one post, all applications submitted by the candidate shall be summarily rejected." Carefully read the TORs for all posts, identify the one post for which you are most eligible and experienced, and apply for that post only.

faq.question: Q6. What is the age limit for District Child Protection Officer (DCPO) in Mission Vatsalya Delhi 2026?
faq.answer: The upper age limit for DCPO is 40 years as on the application closing date (26-06-2026). Similarly, Programme Officer, Protection Officer (IC & NIC), and Legal cum Probation Officer also have an upper age limit of 40 years. Posts like Counselor, Social Worker, Accountant, Data Analyst, ADEO, and Out Reach Worker have an upper age limit of 35 years. Age relaxation as per Delhi State Government rules is applicable for reserved category candidates; additional relaxation is available for candidates already working in WCD, GNCTD.

faq.question: Q7. What is the qualification required for Protection Officer posts in Mission Vatsalya Delhi WCD 2026?
faq.answer: For Protection Officer (Institutional Care) and Protection Officer (Non-Institutional Care), the eligibility is either: (a) Post Graduate degree in Social Work/Sociology/Child Development/Human Rights/Public Administration/Psychology/Psychiatry/Law/Public Health/Community Resource Management with minimum 50% marks, plus 12th with min. 60%; OR (b) Graduate in the above disciplines with minimum 50% marks plus 2 years of post-graduation experience in Women & Child Development/Social Welfare, plus 12th with min. 60%. Computer skills and fluency in English & Hindi are also required for both routes.

faq.question: Q8. What documents are required for Mission Vatsalya Delhi WCD Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Key documents include: educational qualification certificates (12th, graduation, PG degree, LLB, PG Diploma, or Computer Diploma as applicable per post); experience certificates from employers (specifying period, nature of work, and regular paid capacity); age proof (10th certificate); category certificate (SC/ST/OBC/EWS as per Delhi State Govt. format); typing speed certificate (min. 35 WPM for ADEO post); recent passport-size photograph; photo identity proof (Aadhaar/Voter ID); and NOC from present employer (for departmental/government employees). Keep all documents ready before the interview call.
